title: Authorization / Access Denied
description: The user is missing one or more authorization objects required to perform the action. This is the most common support issue in SAP.
steps: Run SU53 immediately as the affected user (or ask them to run it) right after the error occurs — SU53 only stores the most recent failed check.
Note the missing authorization object, field name, and the value that was checked.
Open PFCG and locate the role(s) assigned to the user. Search for the missing authorization object on the Authorization tab.
Add or correct the field value in the role and regenerate the authorization profile.
Re-assign the role in SU01 if it was newly added — profile regeneration alone is usually sufficient for existing assignments.
Ask the user to re-test. If SU53 shows a new failure, repeat for each additional missing object.

title: User Account Locked
description: A user cannot log in because their account is locked, either administratively or due to repeated failed logon attempts.
steps: Go to SU01, enter the username, and open the record in Change mode.
On the Logon Data tab, check the lock status icon and the reason for the lock.
If locked by failed logon attempts, clear the lock and save.
If administratively locked by another admin, investigate the reason before unlocking.
Advise the user to use a new password after unlocking, especially if the lock was due to multiple failed attempts.
Check SM21 system log for the pattern of logon failures if this recurs.

title: Transport Imported but Change Not Visible
description: A transport was imported successfully but the expected changes are not visible in the target system.
steps: Open STMS and check the import log for the transport in the target system's import queue — a green traffic light does not mean error-free.
Verify the correct client was targeted (Customizing transports are client-specific).
Open the transport in SE09 and check the object list — confirm the expected object is included.
Check whether another transport imported after this one overwrote the same object.
If the import log shows errors or warnings, re-import the transport from the STMS queue.
For ABAP programs, check if the program needs to be activated after import.

title: OData Service Not Found / Not Active
description: An OData service returns a 404 or is not accessible. Usually caused by the service not being activated on the Gateway or a stale metadata cache.
steps: Go to /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE and search for the service by name. Check whether it is active.
If not found, add and activate the service, setting the correct system alias.
Check SICF to ensure the ICF node for the service path is active.
Run /IWBEP/CACHE_CLEANUP on the backend to clear the metadata cache.
Run /IWFND/CACHE_CLEANUP on the Gateway frontend.
Test the $metadata URL in a browser to confirm the service is responding.

title: Background Job Failed or Aborted
description: A scheduled background job has failed, aborted, or did not run as expected.
steps: Open SM37 and filter by job name, user, or date range. Identify the failed job.
Open the job log to read the last message — it usually points to the root cause.
If the log references an ABAP exception or short dump, go to ST22 to find the dump.
Check SM21 for system-level errors at the time of failure.
Identify whether the issue is a data problem, missing authorization (check SU53), or program error.
Fix the root cause and reschedule or re-run the job.

title: ABAP Runtime Error / Short Dump
description: An ABAP program terminated with a runtime error. Common causes include type conflicts, null object access, or missing configuration data.
steps: Go to ST22 and find the dump by date/time, user, or program name.
Read the exception description and the active call stack to identify where the error occurred.
Check whether it is a standard SAP program — if so, search SAP Notes (OSS) for the dump type.
If it is a custom program, involve the ABAP developer to fix the logic.
If the dump involves authorization checks, check SU53 for missing authorization objects.

title: FI Document Not Posting
description: A financial document cannot be posted. Common causes include closed posting periods, blocked G/L accounts, missing cost objects, or document balance errors.
steps: Note the exact error message — it usually contains the G/L account, cost center, or period causing the issue.
If the error is 'posting period closed', the fiscal period is locked. Ask FI admins to open the period in OB52.
If the G/L account is blocked for posting, check the account master data and remove the posting block.
If a cost center or cost object is missing or invalid, verify the CO assignment on the line item.
If the document does not balance to zero, check all debit and credit line items.
For recurring errors, check SM21 for update task failures after posting.

title: Payment Run Not Picking Up Open Items
description: The F110 automatic payment run is not selecting expected vendor invoices. Usually caused by incorrect payment parameters, blocked invoices, or missing payment method configuration.
steps: Open F110 and review the payment run parameters: company code, payment method, vendor selection, and due date.
Check FBL1N for the vendor's open items — confirm the invoice is open and not already cleared.
In FBL1N, check the payment block indicator on the invoice (a payment block prevents selection).
Confirm the payment method on the invoice matches what is configured in the payment run.
Check that the invoice due date falls within the next payment date range in F110.
Review the payment log from the last run for exclusion reasons.

title: Sales Order Blocked
description: A sales order has a delivery block, billing block, or credit block that prevents it from being processed further.
steps: Open VA03 and navigate to the header. Check the Delivery Block and Billing Block fields.
If there is a credit block, check the customer's credit status — the credit manager must release it.
For a delivery block, understand the reason (e.g., incomplete data, manual hold) and remove it in VA02.
For a billing block on a line item, check item conditions and pricing — blocks often indicate a pricing issue.
After removing blocks, check whether downstream documents (delivery, invoice) can now be created.

title: Goods Receipt Quantity or Value Mismatch
description: A goods receipt does not match the purchase order quantity or value, or stock levels are not updated as expected.
steps: Open MIGO and display the material document to see what was actually posted.
Compare the posted quantity and unit of measure against the PO line in ME23N.
Check MB51 for the material document history to see all movements for the material.
If a GR was posted with the wrong quantity, post a reversal in MIGO (movement type 102) and repost correctly.
Use MMBE to verify the current stock balance reflects the expected quantity.
If the GR is missing entirely, check SM37 for any automated GR jobs that may have failed.

title: Timesheet Entry Missing or Not Saved
description: A user's CATS timesheet entry is not visible or appears to not have been saved.
steps: Open CAT2 for the affected employee and date — check whether the entry exists and was saved and released.
Unsaved or unreleased entries will not transfer to HR. Look for entries in 'In Process' status.
Check SM37 for the CATS transfer job (RPTCAT** variants) to confirm it ran and completed successfully.
If the entry should have transferred to HR, use PT50 to check whether the absence/attendance is visible.
If entries are systematically missing, check the CATS transfer job schedule and last run log.

title: Workflow Stuck or Not Progressing
description: A workflow instance is not advancing, usually because a workitem has no assigned agent, the responsible user cannot action it, or the workflow hit an error.
steps: Go to SWI5 and search by task or status to find the stuck workitem.
Open the workitem and check whether an agent is assigned — no agent means no one is responsible.
Use SWIA to execute, forward, or complete the workitem administratively to unblock the process.
Check the workflow log (SWI1) to understand where and why the process stalled.
If no agent is assigned systematically, review the role resolution and org data in PPOME.

title: RFC Connection Failing
description: An RFC (Remote Function Call) to another SAP system or external service is failing. Causes include wrong connection parameters, the target system being unavailable, or authorization issues.
steps: Open SM59 and find the RFC destination by name. Use the Test Connection and Remote Login buttons to diagnose.
Check the hostname, system number, and logon credentials configured in the destination.
Test the network connection independently (ping / traceroute to the target host).
Check whether the target system is running — verify in the target system's SM21 or with the basis team.
For Trusted RFC connections, verify the trust settings are configured on both systems.
Check SM21 for RFC-related errors at the time of the failure.

title: Leave Quota Exceeded or Missing
description: An employee cannot book leave because their quota is exhausted, not yet accrued, or missing entirely.
steps: Open PT50 for the employee and check the absence quota balance for the relevant absence type.
If the quota is zero or missing, check PT60 to see when time evaluation last ran and whether it accrued the quota.
Verify the employee's employment status and working schedule — incorrect schedules prevent accrual.
If quota should have been generated but was not, re-run time evaluation in PT60 for the employee.
For manual quota corrections, an HR admin can add or adjust quotas directly via PA30 (infotype 2006).

title: IDoc Failed or Stuck in Error
description: An inbound or outbound IDoc failed during processing. Common causes include missing master data, partner profile issues, or application posting errors.
steps: Open WE02 and locate the failed IDoc by status, message type, partner, or time range.
Review the status records and error text to identify the actual reason for failure.
Use WE05 if you need broader filtering across message types or partners.
Fix the root cause before retrying the message.
Reprocess the IDoc in BD87.
Confirm the IDoc reaches a successful final status and the expected business document is created.

title: Update Task Failed / Data Not Saved
description: The transaction appeared to save, but the database update failed in the update task. This usually requires checking SM13 and related technical logs.
steps: Go to SM13 and search for failed update requests around the time of the issue.
Open the failed update request and review the error details.
If there is a related ABAP runtime error, inspect it in ST22.
Check SM21 for additional system-level information at the same timestamp.
Fix the underlying issue and repeat the business transaction if necessary.

title: Purchase Order Not Released
description: A purchase order is waiting for approval or not progressing through the release strategy.
steps: Open ME23N and check the current release status of the purchase order.
Verify whether the PO was changed after a previous approval, which may have reset the release.
Confirm the correct release strategy and approver are being determined.
Use ME29N to release the PO if you have the proper authority.
Re-display the document and confirm the release status has updated.

title: Vendor Invoice Blocked
description: A vendor invoice is blocked due to quantity or price variance, invoice verification rules, or payment restrictions.
steps: Find the invoice in FBL1N and confirm its current open and block status.
Review the invoice details in MIRO and compare them with the purchase order and goods receipt.
Resolve the underlying business variance first.
Release the blocked invoice in MRBR if appropriate.
Verify that the invoice is now eligible for payment.

title: Workflow Item Missing from Inbox
description: A workflow workitem exists or should exist, but the user cannot see it in their SAP inbox.
steps: Check SBWP to confirm the workitem is not already present in the user's inbox.
Use SWI5 to search for the workflow item by task, user, or status.
Verify whether an agent was assigned to the workitem.
If the item is stuck, use SWIA for administrative processing.
Ask the user to refresh the inbox and confirm visibility after the fix.

title: Material Missing or Not Extended
description: A material cannot be found in the expected organizational level or required view. The record may not exist or may not be extended to the plant or storage-relevant views.
steps: Open MM03 and check whether the material exists at all.
Verify whether the required plant or view is maintained for the material.
If the material exists but lacks required data, extend or update it in MM02.
If the material does not exist, create it in MM01 according to business rules.
Re-test the original process once the material master is complete.

title: Spool Output Missing or Not Printed
description: A report or form appears to have run, but the expected spool output is missing, stuck, or sent to the wrong device.
steps: Confirm the report or background job actually completed successfully.
Search SP01 for the spool request by user, program, or date.
Check the spool status and target output device.
Verify whether the output was sent to the correct printer or logical device.
Reprint or forward the existing spool request if appropriate.

title: Record Locked by Another User or Process
description: A business object or table entry is locked and cannot be changed. This may be a valid active lock or a stale lock left behind by a failed process.
steps: Search SM12 for the lock entry by user, object, or key.
Confirm whether the locking session is still active.
Check SM21 if the lock appears to be stale after a failure.
Ask the owning user or team to exit cleanly if possible.
Delete the lock only when you are sure it is no longer in active use.

title: qRFC Queue Stuck or Not Processing
description: A queued RFC message is stuck in outbound or inbound processing. Common causes include application errors, RFC issues, or dependent queues waiting to be cleared.
steps: Check SMQ1 in the source system to see whether the outbound queue is stuck.
Check SMQ2 in the target system to see whether the message arrived but is waiting there.
Review SLG1 for application-level error details.
Verify SM59 if the queue issue appears to be communication-related.
Fix the root cause before restarting or unlocking the queue.

title: Purchase Requisition Not Released
description: A purchase requisition is waiting for approval or not progressing through its release strategy.
steps: Open ME53N and review the release status of the requisition.
Check whether document changes reset the release status.
Confirm the correct release strategy and approver determination.
Use ME54N to release the requisition if you are authorized.
Re-display the requisition and confirm the updated release status.

title: Pricing Not Determined or Incorrect
description: A sales order has missing or incorrect pricing because the condition record, validity, or determination logic is not matching as expected.
steps: Open the sales order in VA03 and identify which pricing condition is missing or incorrect.
Check the sales area, customer, material, and pricing date on the order.
Use VK13 to verify that a valid condition record exists.
Confirm whether pricing procedure and access sequence logic are correct.
Re-price the order in VA02 after correcting the underlying issue.

title: Outbound Delivery Not Created
description: A sales order is not resulting in an outbound delivery because of blocks, missing confirmations, delivery relevance issues, or incorrect dates.
steps: Open VA03 and check the sales order status and any delivery blocks.
Review delivery-relevant dates, confirmed quantity, and schedule lines.
Try manual delivery creation in VL01N if appropriate.
Check whether a delivery already exists in VL03N.
If the delivery exists, continue processing in VL02N.

title: FI Posting Period Closed
description: A financial posting cannot be made because the accounting period is closed for the relevant company code or account type.
steps: Capture the posting date, company code, and account type from the error.
Check OB52 to confirm whether the period is open for that account type.
Verify that the posting date is correct and falls into the intended period.
Open the period only if you are authorized and business approval exists.
Repeat the posting once the period issue is resolved.

title: Employee Missing from Organizational Structure
description: An employee exists in HR master data but is not visible or correctly placed in the organizational structure.
steps: Check the employee's organizational assignment data in PA30.
Open PPOME to verify the org unit and position assignment.
Use PPOSE to confirm whether the employee appears in the expected structure.
Correct the org assignment or holder relationship if necessary.
Validate the final placement in both PPOME and PA20.

title: Leave Quota Missing or Incorrect
description: An employee's leave balance is missing, too low, or not updated because accrual or master data is incomplete or incorrect.
steps: Check the employee's quota balances in PT50.
Review or rerun PT60 if accrual should already have happened.
Check relevant HR master data in PA30.
Correct quota data in infotype 2006 if a manual adjustment is required.
Recheck the balance in PT50 after correction.

title: New Employee Setup Incomplete
description: A new employee was started in HR but required infotypes or organizational placement are incomplete, so downstream processes do not work correctly.
steps: Review the completed hiring action in PA40.
Check PA30 for missing or incomplete infotypes.
Verify organizational placement in PPOME or PPOSE.
Correct any missing data and perform a final validation in PA20.
Retest the affected downstream process after the employee record is complete.

title: Slow Transaction or General Performance Issue
description: A user reports that SAP is slow or a specific transaction has poor response time. The issue may be system-wide, server-specific, or caused by expensive processing.
steps: Start with ST03N to determine whether the issue is broad or isolated.
Check active work processes in SM50 and SM66 for long-running or blocked sessions.
Confirm whether one application server is affected using SM51.
Run ST05 if you need a detailed SQL or RFC trace for the problematic transaction.
Differentiate between workload, application design, and infrastructure causes before taking action.

title: OData Service Active but Failing at Runtime
description: An OData service is active and reachable but fails during execution due to backend logic, model mismatch, or runtime exceptions.
steps: Open /IWFND/ERROR_LOG and locate the failed request.
Review the technical details and backend exception text.
Check SEGW if the issue points to service implementation or runtime generation.
Review ST22 at the same timestamp for backend dumps.
Retest the service after fixing the identified cause.

title: SAP Email Not Sent
description: An email or outbound SAPconnect message is waiting, failed, or never left the system. Causes include send job, node configuration, or data issues.
steps: Open SOST and find the send request.
Check whether the request is waiting, failed, or already processed.
Review the request details and error text.
Check SCOT if the issue appears to be configuration or scheduling-related.
Retry or monitor the message after correction.

title: Workflow Failed During Execution
description: A workflow started but failed during processing due to agent issues, business data problems, or technical exceptions.
steps: Open SWI1 and locate the failed workflow instance.
Review the workflow log to find the failing step.
Use SWI5 to identify related workitems and statuses.
Determine whether the issue is routing, data, or execution-related.
Use SWIA if administrative intervention is required.

title: Sales Order Credit Block
description: A sales order is blocked by credit management because of credit exposure, overdue receivables, or customer risk controls.
steps: Check VA03 to confirm the order is blocked specifically for credit reasons.
Review the customer's credit situation and business context.
Use VKM3 to review the blocked document in credit management.
Release the order only if business approval exists.
Recheck the order afterwards to confirm the block is gone.

title: MM Posting Blocked by Closed Period
description: A goods movement or materials posting cannot be made because the MM period is not open for the intended posting date.
steps: Capture the exact movement and posting date from the error.
Check MMPV to determine the active MM period.
Confirm whether the posting date is appropriate for the intended business event.
Advance the MM period only if authorized and appropriate.
Retry the posting after the period issue is resolved.

title: Incorrect FI Document Needs Reversal
description: A financial document was posted incorrectly and needs to be reversed in a controlled way.
steps: Display the document in FB03 and confirm it is the one to reverse.
Check whether it is eligible for reversal and whether period restrictions apply.
Reverse the document in FB08.
Confirm the reversal document was created correctly.
Review balances afterwards if needed.

title: Manual Vendor Payment Needed
description: A vendor invoice must be paid manually outside the automatic payment run process.
steps: Review the vendor's open items in FBL1N.
Validate the invoice details in FB03 if necessary.
Post the manual outgoing payment in F-53.
Confirm the invoice is cleared afterwards in FBL1N.

title: Manual Customer Payment Needed
description: A customer payment must be posted manually and matched to open receivables.
steps: Review the customer's open items in FBL5N.
Validate the invoice details if necessary.
Post the incoming payment in F-28.
Confirm the receivable is cleared afterwards.

title: Timesheet Waiting for Approval
description: A time entry was submitted but has not yet been approved, so it is not available for downstream processing or balances.
steps: Check the submitted time entry status in CAT2.
Review pending approvals in CAT7.
Confirm the correct approver or workflow is involved.
Approve the entry if appropriate.
Validate the downstream result afterwards.

title: Employee Time Data Incorrect
description: An employee's HR time data such as absences or attendances is incorrect and needs correction through the proper infotype process.
steps: Identify the incorrect time record and date range.
Open the relevant time infotype in PA61.
Correct the record using the proper HR maintenance process.
Review downstream balances or calculations in PT50.
Rerun PT60 if recalculation is required.

title: tRFC Call Stuck or Failing
description: A transactional RFC call is waiting, failing repeatedly, or not reaching the target system. Common causes include broken destinations, logon issues, or temporary communication failures.
steps: Open SM58 and locate the failed or waiting entry.
Review the destination, function call, and exact error text.
Test the destination in SM59 to check connection and logon behavior.
Review SM21 around the same time for supporting technical errors.
Fix the destination, authorization, or target-system problem before retrying.
Reprocess the entry in SM58 and confirm it clears successfully.

pattern: access issue not explained by su53
title: Authorization Failure Needs Deeper Trace
description: An access issue still cannot be explained clearly after checking SU53. This usually means a deeper authorization trace is needed to see the exact failed checks.
steps: Check SU53 first to capture the latest failed authorization check.
If SU53 is incomplete or unclear, activate an authorization trace in ST01 for the affected user.
Have the user repeat the exact failing action while the trace is active.
Analyze the trace results to identify the missing object and field values.
Adjust the relevant role content in PFCG based on the traced evidence.
Retest the transaction after the role correction.

title: Table Cannot Be Maintained in SM30
description: A table or view cannot be maintained through SM30 because no maintenance dialog exists, the wrong path is being used, or the user lacks the required authorization.
steps: Open SM30 and enter the exact table or view name.
Review the returned error or restriction message carefully.
Use SE16N to verify the correct table contents and key values.
Check whether the object should be maintained through SPRO or another controlled transaction instead of SM30.
Confirm the user has the right maintenance authorization and client context.

title: Document Missing from Billing Due List
description: A sales or delivery document is expected to be invoiced but does not appear in the billing due list. Causes often include billing blocks, incomplete delivery processing, or billing relevance issues.
steps: Open VF04 and search using the correct sales, delivery, and organizational selection criteria.
Check VL03N to confirm the delivery is complete and goods issue has been posted if required.
Review billing status and any billing blocks in VA03.
Confirm the document is actually billing-relevant for the intended process.
Try manual billing creation in VF01 if appropriate to surface the direct blocking error.

title: SSL Certificate or Trust Error
description: A secure connection fails because SAP does not trust the certificate chain or the relevant certificate is missing or expired.
steps: Open STRUST and identify the relevant SSL client or server trust store.
Check whether the required certificate chain is present and still valid.
Review whether the certificate is expired, missing, or incomplete.
Retest the related connection in SM59 or the affected service path in SICF.
Import or replace the required certificates and test again.

title: Automatic Account Determination Missing
description: An MM-related posting fails because SAP cannot determine the required G/L account automatically. This is usually caused by missing or mismatched OBYC configuration or material valuation data.
steps: Capture the exact posting error, movement type, plant, and material involved.
Check MM03 to review the material's valuation class and accounting-relevant setup.
Open OBYC and verify the relevant transaction key and account assignment entry.
Confirm the valuation grouping code and account determination context match the posting scenario.
Repeat the posting after correcting the missing or incorrect account determination.

pattern: material not maintained for org level
title: Material Not Extended to Required Organizational Level
description: A process fails because the material exists, but the required plant, storage, purchasing, sales, or accounting view is missing for the organizational level being used.
steps: Open MM03 and check which views and organizational levels exist for the material.
Confirm whether the failing process needs a plant, sales area, purchasing, or accounting extension that is currently missing.
Use MM02 to extend the material to the required views and organizational levels.
Review the business process context to ensure the correct plant or sales area is being used.
Repeat the original transaction after the material extension is complete.

pattern: quota not updated after time entry
title: Time Evaluation Not Run or Not Updated
description: Employee time results, overtime, or quota effects are missing because time evaluation has not run, failed, or not processed the expected employee data.
steps: Open PT60 and check whether time evaluation ran for the affected employee and period.
Confirm the employee's time data exists correctly in CAT2 or the relevant HR infotypes.
Review whether master data in PA30 supports the expected time evaluation process.
Rerun PT60 for the employee or relevant period if appropriate.
Check PT50 or downstream results again after time evaluation completes.

title: Output Determination Not Triggered
description: A document is created successfully, but the expected output message such as print, email, or EDI is not determined at all.
steps: Confirm the business document was created successfully and identify the expected output type.
Check whether any message record exists on the document itself.
Review NACE to confirm the application, output type, access sequence, and requirement routine are configured correctly.
Check whether a valid condition record exists for the output type using the relevant maintenance transaction such as VV31 or MN04.
Verify that document data such as sales area, partner, language, and communication type match the condition record.
Retest the document after correcting the missing determination condition or configuration.

title: Document Incompletion Blocking Processing
description: A sales or delivery document is incomplete and cannot proceed to the next step because required data is missing.
steps: Open the document in change or display mode and review the incompletion log.
Identify which required fields are missing at header or item level.
Check whether the missing field comes from master data, partner determination, or manual entry.
If the issue is recurring, review incompletion procedure settings in customizing.
Complete the required fields and save the document.
Retest the blocked downstream step such as delivery or billing creation.

title: Availability Check Failed / No Confirmed Quantity
description: A sales order or delivery-relevant document does not confirm quantity because ATP or availability check cannot find sufficient stock or receipt coverage.
steps: Check the affected item in the sales order and confirm that the confirmed quantity is zero or too low.
Use CO09 to review the ATP situation for the material and plant.
Check current stock in MMBE and planned receipts or requirements in MD04.
Verify that the requested delivery date, plant, and checking rule are correct.
If the issue is configuration-related, review availability check settings for the material and document type.
Retest the order after stock, dates, or ATP settings are corrected.

title: Batch Determination Failed
description: A batch-managed material cannot be processed as expected because automatic batch determination did not find or assign a valid batch.
steps: Confirm that the material is batch-managed and identify where batch determination was expected to happen.
Check whether eligible batches actually exist and have unrestricted stock.
Review batch attributes, classification values, and selection criteria relevant to the strategy.
Verify the batch search strategy and condition record configuration.
Check whether the issue is caused by missing stock, missing classification data, or wrong strategy assignment.
Retest the transaction after correcting batch data or determination settings.

title: No Valid Source of Supply Found
description: A purchasing process cannot continue because SAP cannot determine a valid source of supply for the material and plant.
steps: Review the requisition or purchasing document context to identify the material, plant, and required vendor selection.
Check whether a valid source list entry exists for the material and plant.
Verify whether quota arrangement, info record, or contract data should have been used.
Check validity dates and blocking indicators on source-related records.
If source determination is expected automatically, review the relevant purchasing configuration and master data.
Retry the process after maintaining a valid source of supply.

title: Purchasing Info Record Missing or Incomplete
description: A purchasing document cannot determine expected vendor-material data because the purchasing info record is missing, incomplete, or not valid.
steps: Identify the vendor, material, purchasing organization, and plant involved.
Use ME13 to check whether a purchasing info record exists for the required combination.
Verify price, order unit, validity, and purchasing organization data.
If the record exists but is incomplete, maintain the missing fields in ME12.
If it does not exist, create it in ME11 according to business rules.
Retest the purchasing document after the info record is corrected.

title: Vendor Confirmation Missing
description: A purchase order expects vendor confirmation data, but no valid order acknowledgment or confirmation record exists.
steps: Open the purchase order and identify whether confirmation control is expected on the item.
Check whether a confirmation record exists and whether the dates and quantities are valid.
Review confirmation control settings and vendor communication process.
Determine whether confirmation should be entered manually, via EDI, or through another integration path.
Maintain or trigger the missing confirmation as appropriate.
Recheck the PO item afterwards to confirm the confirmation is now visible.

pattern: cannot save requisition due to account assignment
title: Account Assignment Missing or Invalid
description: A purchasing, service, or logistics document cannot be posted or saved because the required account assignment is missing, invalid, or inconsistent.
steps: Capture the exact error and identify the account assignment category in the document item.
Check whether the required CO object such as cost center, order, WBS element, or asset is present and valid.
Verify the organizational context such as company code, controlling area, and validity dates.
Check whether the receiving object is blocked, expired, or not allowed for the document type.
Correct the account assignment values in the document or master data as needed.
Retest the save or posting after the correction.

title: Number Range Missing or Exhausted
description: A document or object cannot be created because the assigned number range interval is missing, invalid, or exhausted.
steps: Capture the exact message and identify the object that failed to get a number.
Check the relevant number range object and interval settings.
Verify that the correct interval exists for the company code, year, or document type context.
Check whether the interval is exhausted or locked.
Create or extend the interval only according to business rules and authorization.
Retry the original transaction after the number range issue is resolved.

pattern: message not generated from master change
title: Change Pointer Not Created
description: Expected outbound processing based on master data or transaction changes does not start because change pointers were not created.
steps: Confirm that the business change really occurred and should create an outbound message.
Check whether global change pointer creation is active.
Verify that the relevant message type is enabled for change pointers.
Inspect change pointer records to see whether entries were created.
If entries are missing, review activation and object-specific settings.
Recreate or trigger processing after correcting the change pointer configuration.

pattern: message type not allowed for partner
title: Partner Profile Missing or Incomplete
description: An IDoc or ALE message cannot be processed correctly because the relevant partner profile is missing or incomplete.
steps: Identify the sender, receiver, direction, and message type involved in the failure.
Open WE20 and verify that the relevant partner profile exists.
Check the inbound or outbound parameters for the required message type and process code.
Confirm that port and model configuration are aligned with the partner profile.
Maintain the missing or incorrect partner profile settings.
Reprocess the message after the profile is corrected.

title: BDC Session Failed in SM35
description: A classic batch input session was created but failed during processing because of screen changes, data issues, or missing authorizations.
steps: Open SM35 and locate the failed batch input session.
Review the session log and identify the screen or field where processing failed.
Check whether the issue was caused by changed screen flow, invalid input data, or missing authorization.
If the recording is outdated, compare the current transaction flow with the original batch input design.
Correct the input data or batch input logic as needed.
Reprocess the session after the issue is fixed.

title: Report Variant Incorrect or Outdated
description: A report or background job runs, but the result is wrong because the assigned variant contains incorrect, outdated, or incomplete selection values.
steps: Identify the exact program and variant used by the user or background job.
Review the variant values and compare them with the expected business scope.
Check whether dynamic variables or TVARVC references are still valid.
Confirm whether the wrong result is due to stale dates, company code values, or filters.
Update the variant with correct values and save it under proper control.
Rerun the report or job and compare the new result.

pattern: function returned success but no document
title: BAPI Executed but Data Was Not Committed
description: A custom integration or program calls a BAPI successfully, but the expected data is not saved because the LUW was not committed properly.
steps: Confirm that the BAPI returned success or a document number without a hard error.
Check whether the program executed a proper COMMIT WORK or BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT afterward.
Review SM13 if the update should have happened but failed later in update task processing.
Check custom code or integration middleware for missing commit handling.
Correct the transaction handling in the calling logic.
Retest and verify that the created object is now actually saved.

title: Electronic Bank Statement Not Posted Correctly
description: An electronic bank statement is uploaded or expected, but posting or interpretation fails because of format, account symbols, or interpretation issues.
steps: Confirm that the bank statement file was uploaded successfully and identify the statement number and account.
Check whether interpretation or posting errors were generated during processing.
Review FEBAN for items requiring manual clarification or reprocessing.
Verify account symbols, external transaction mapping, and posting rules.
Correct the mapping or statement interpretation issue.
Reprocess the statement items and confirm the posting result.

title: Foreign Trade or Compliance Data Missing
description: A sales, delivery, or purchasing process is blocked or incomplete because required foreign trade, compliance, or customs-relevant data is missing.
steps: Identify which document and item triggered the foreign trade or compliance-related message.
Check whether the material master contains the required trade or customs data.
Review the customer, vendor, plant, and destination country context of the transaction.
Check whether the missing data belongs in material master, business partner master, or document-level fields.
Maintain the missing compliance or foreign trade data according to business rules.
Retest the blocked process afterwards.

title: Service Entry Sheet Cannot Be Accepted
description: A service entry sheet exists but cannot be accepted because of quantity, value, release, or authorization issues.
steps: Open the service entry sheet and identify the exact acceptance error or status.
Compare the entered service quantities and values with the related PO limits and service lines.
Check whether the entry sheet requires release or additional approval before acceptance.
Verify account assignment and authorization context if acceptance is blocked unexpectedly.
Correct the business inconsistency or release issue.
Retry the acceptance and confirm the status updates correctly.

title: Work Center Missing or Invalid
description: A routing, task list, or production order cannot process correctly because the referenced work center or resource is missing, invalid, or not properly assigned.
steps: Identify the routing or order operation where the work center issue occurs.
Check whether the referenced work center exists and is valid for the required dates and plant context.
Review whether capacity, cost center, or usage assignments are incomplete.
Correct the routing or work center master data if needed.
Recheck the task list or production order after the correction.
Retest the business process that failed.

pattern: cannot create production order no routing
title: Routing or Task List Missing
description: A manufacturing process cannot continue because SAP cannot find a valid routing or task list for the material, plant, and usage context.
steps: Identify the material, plant, and order type involved.
Check whether a valid routing or task list exists for the required usage and validity dates.
If production versions are used, confirm the correct version points to the right BOM and routing.
Review material master and plant-level production-relevant data.
Maintain or correct the missing routing or version assignment as needed.
Retry the failed order creation or planning step.

title: Production Order Confirmation Missing
description: A production order or operation is expected to have confirmation data, but no valid yield or time confirmation was posted.
steps: Check the production order and identify which operation or phase is expected to be confirmed.
Review existing confirmations and status at order and operation level.
Determine whether the confirmation should have been posted manually, automatically, or through integration.
Correct the confirmation data using the proper transaction if business rules allow it.
Recheck order status and downstream postings after the confirmation is entered.
Validate whether goods receipt, backflush, or settlement dependencies are now unblocked.

title: Settlement Rule Missing or Invalid
description: An order, project, or cost object cannot be settled because the settlement rule is missing, incomplete, or invalid.
steps: Identify the cost object and capture the exact settlement error.
Check whether a settlement rule exists and whether it has a valid receiver.
Review settlement profile, validity dates, and distribution percentages.
Confirm that the receiver object is still valid and open for settlement.
Correct the settlement rule in the appropriate maintenance transaction.
Retry settlement after the rule is fixed.

title: Cost Center Invalid, Expired, or Blocked
description: Posting fails because the referenced cost center is expired, blocked, outside validity, or not valid in the controlling context.
steps: Capture the exact cost center and posting date from the error.
Check the cost center validity dates, lock indicators, and controlling area assignment.
Verify whether the posting date falls inside the active validity period.
Confirm that the cost center is allowed for the business process and company code context.
Correct the master data or choose the appropriate valid cost center.
Retest the posting afterwards.

title: Required Partner Function Missing
description: A sales or purchasing document cannot proceed correctly because one or more required partner functions were not determined.
steps: Open the affected document and identify which partner function is missing.
Check the partner tab at header and item level.
Review the customer or business partner master data to confirm the required partner function is maintained.
Verify whether partner determination procedure customizing is correct for the document type.
Maintain the missing partner function in master data or the document if business rules allow it.
Retest the process after the partner data is complete.

title: Incorrect Ship-To or Delivery Address
description: A document uses the wrong delivery address because master data, partner assignment, or document-level address handling is incorrect.
steps: Open the sales order or delivery and review the ship-to and address details.
Determine whether the wrong address came from master data or was changed manually in the document.
Check the ship-to party master data and address records.
Confirm whether the correct partner function was determined.
Correct the address in the proper source location according to business rules.
Validate downstream documents after the correction.

title: Route Not Determined
description: A delivery or sales process cannot determine a valid route because shipping conditions, departure zone, transportation group, or destination data do not match route determination rules.
steps: Check the document for shipping point, shipping conditions, transportation group, and destination country/zone.
Confirm the material and customer data contain the required route-relevant fields.
Review route determination customizing and ensure a valid route exists for the combination.
Correct missing master data or route configuration.
Redetermine the route in the document if needed.
Retest delivery creation or shipment planning afterwards.

pattern: cannot create delivery no shipping point
title: Shipping Point Not Determined
description: A delivery-relevant process fails because SAP cannot determine the shipping point from plant, loading group, and shipping conditions.
steps: Check the sales order item for plant, loading group, and shipping conditions.
Verify the material master contains the required loading group and shipping data.
Confirm shipping point determination customizing covers the combination used.
Correct master data or configuration gaps.
Reprocess the order or create the delivery again.
Confirm the shipping point is now determined correctly.

title: Picking Not Confirmed
description: An outbound delivery cannot move forward because required picking confirmation has not been completed or is inconsistent.
steps: Check the delivery status and determine whether picking is still open.
Review picked quantity versus delivery quantity at item level.
If Warehouse Management is used, confirm whether transfer order creation and confirmation are complete.
Identify whether stock, storage location, or batch issues blocked picking.
Complete or correct the picking process.
Recheck delivery status after the picking confirmation.

title: Post Goods Issue Failed
description: A delivery cannot be completed because post goods issue fails due to stock, batch, period, or account determination issues.
steps: Capture the exact PGI error text from the delivery.
Check stock, batch, and storage location availability for the delivery items.
Verify whether the MM posting period and account determination are valid.
Confirm picking and delivery status are complete enough for PGI.
Fix the underlying logistics or accounting issue.
Retry post goods issue and confirm the document status updates.

title: Billing Document Needs Cancellation
description: A billing document was created incorrectly and needs to be cancelled in a controlled way before correction or rebilling.
steps: Display the billing document and confirm it is the one that should be cancelled.
Check whether it has already transferred to accounting and whether cancellation is still allowed.
Cancel the billing document in VF11 if appropriate.
Confirm the cancellation document was created successfully.
Review whether a corrected billing document must now be created.
Validate downstream accounting impact after cancellation.

title: Purchase Order History Inconsistent
description: Purchase order history does not match expected goods receipts, invoice receipts, or subsequent postings, causing confusion in follow-on processing.
steps: Display the PO history and compare expected versus actual goods and invoice events.
Check material documents and invoice documents related to the PO item.
Identify whether reversals, duplicate postings, or blocked invoices caused the mismatch.
Validate movement types and quantities in the related postings.
Correct the underlying goods or invoice posting where needed.
Recheck PO history after corrections.

title: GR/IR Balance Not Clearing
description: The GR/IR clearing account still shows a balance because goods receipt and invoice receipt postings do not match correctly.
steps: Identify the affected PO item and compare GR and IR quantities and values.
Review related material and invoice documents.
Check whether price differences, quantity differences, or wrong postings caused the residual balance.
Determine whether additional goods receipt, invoice correction, or GR/IR clearing action is needed.
Use MR11 or the appropriate correction process according to business rules.
Recheck the GR/IR balance afterwards.

title: Subcontracting Components Missing or Incorrect
description: A subcontracting process fails or gives wrong stock results because required components were not staged, issued, or maintained correctly.
steps: Identify the subcontracting PO item and expected component list.
Review component stock and staging situation for the supplying plant.
Check whether the necessary issue posting to vendor stock happened correctly.
Validate BOM and component assignment for the subcontracting material.
Correct stock staging or component master data as needed.
Retest the subcontracting follow-on process.

title: Material Valuation Data Missing
description: A material-related posting fails because the material master lacks required accounting or valuation data such as valuation class or price control.
steps: Check the material's accounting and valuation views in the correct organizational level.
Confirm valuation class, price control, and valuation area are maintained as required.
Verify whether the material has been extended to the required valuation level.
Review whether account determination depends on the missing valuation data.
Maintain the missing valuation information according to business rules.
Retry the posting after the correction.

title: Material Price Missing or Incorrect
description: A material has incorrect valuation pricing such as zero standard price, wrong moving average, or inconsistent price control, affecting postings and reporting.
steps: Review the material valuation data and identify the current price control and valuation price.
Determine whether the issue is master data, costing release, or posting-history related.
Check whether the material should use standard price or moving average price.
Correct the price using the approved business process such as MR21 or costing release where applicable.
Review any recent postings that may have caused the inconsistency.
Retest the affected process or report.

title: Cost Estimate Missing or Not Released
description: A process depending on standard cost or costing data fails because a valid cost estimate does not exist or was not released.
steps: Identify the material, plant, and costing variant involved.
Check whether a valid cost estimate exists for the required period.
Confirm whether the estimate was marked and released if standard cost is needed.
Review master data or BOM/routing issues if costing cannot be generated.
Create or release the cost estimate using the approved costing process.
Recheck the affected posting or valuation result afterwards.

title: Asset Master Missing or Incomplete
description: An asset-related process fails because the asset master record is missing, invalid, or not fully maintained.
steps: Capture the asset number and company code involved in the failure.
Check whether the asset master exists and is active.
Review required fields such as asset class, depreciation areas, and capitalization relevance.
Confirm the asset is valid for the intended posting scenario.
Create or correct the asset master as needed.
Retry the posting or process afterwards.

title: Asset Accounting Posting Failed
description: An asset accounting posting fails because of invalid asset master setup, transaction type, depreciation area settings, or period-related issues.
steps: Review the exact asset accounting error and identify the asset and transaction type involved.
Check whether the asset master and depreciation areas are correctly maintained.
Verify whether account determination and transaction type settings support the intended posting.
Confirm the fiscal period and asset value date are valid.
Correct the relevant asset configuration or master data issue.
Retry the asset posting after the correction.

title: Depreciation Not Run or Not Posted
description: Expected depreciation postings are missing because depreciation calculation or posting has not run correctly.
steps: Identify the company code, period, and affected asset or asset class.
Check whether periodic depreciation posting was executed for the required period.
Review job logs if depreciation runs in background.
Confirm the asset carries planned depreciation values in the current fiscal year.
Correct any period, master data, or run-control issue.
Rerun depreciation posting if appropriate and verify the result.

title: Dunning Run Not Picking Up Items
description: A dunning run does not select expected overdue receivables because of dunning data, block indicators, or run parameters.
steps: Check whether the customer open items are actually overdue and still open.
Review dunning data in customer master and on the invoice itself.
Confirm there is no dunning block on the account or document.
Review the run date, interval, and company code selection in the dunning run.
Correct missing dunning settings or remove inappropriate blocks.
Execute or repeat the dunning run and review the proposal.

title: Tax Code Missing or Invalid
description: Posting fails because the tax code is missing, not valid for the transaction, or not configured correctly in the relevant tax procedure.
steps: Capture the exact tax-related error and identify the transaction context.
Check whether the tax code exists and is valid in the company code and tax procedure context.
Review whether the document type and posting scenario allow the selected tax code.
Confirm the tax code is assigned to the right rates and accounts.
Correct the tax code or document entry as needed.
Retry the posting afterwards.

title: Open Item Clearing Not Possible
description: Open items cannot be cleared because values, currencies, assignment data, or document status do not match the clearing scenario.
steps: Identify the exact open items expected to clear together.
Check whether the items are all still open and in the same account context.
Compare currencies, amounts, assignment references, and posting dates.
Determine whether a residual item, partial payment, or write-off process is required instead.
Correct the business mismatch or use the appropriate clearing method.
Retry the clearing process afterwards.

title: House Bank or Bank Account Determination Failed
description: A payment process fails because no valid house bank or house bank account can be determined.
steps: Review the payment method, company code, and bank determination context in the payment run.
Check whether a valid house bank and account exist for the required currency and payment method.
Verify bank determination customizing and ranking order.
Confirm the vendor or customer bank and payment setup aligns with the selected payment method.
Maintain the missing house bank assignment or payment configuration.
Repeat the payment proposal after the correction.

title: Payment Medium File Not Created
description: A payment run completes, but the expected payment medium file or output is not created because of payment medium configuration or formatting issues.
steps: Confirm the payment run completed and payment documents were created.
Check whether payment medium creation is expected as part of the run variant.
Review payment medium format and house bank configuration.
Check logs for formatting, path, or output generation issues.
Correct the payment medium setup or output path problem.
Regenerate the payment medium and verify the file is produced.

pattern: cost center not assigned to hierarchy
title: Cost Center Missing from Standard Hierarchy
description: Controlling setup or reporting is affected because a cost center is not assigned correctly into the standard hierarchy.
steps: Identify the cost center and controlling area involved.
Check whether the cost center exists and is valid.
Review the standard hierarchy and confirm the expected node assignment.
Determine whether the cost center was created without proper hierarchy placement.
Assign it correctly within the standard hierarchy according to governance.
Retest the report or posting scenario afterwards.

title: Internal Order Not Open for Posting
description: Posting fails because the referenced internal order is closed, locked, or otherwise not open for the intended business transaction.
steps: Capture the internal order number from the posting error.
Check the current system and user status of the order.
Review whether the order is technically complete, closed, or locked against actual postings.
Confirm whether the order is still valid for the posting date and business purpose.
Reopen or replace the order only if business rules allow it.
Retry the posting after the order status issue is resolved.

title: WBS Element Status Blocking Posting
description: A posting or logistics process fails because the referenced WBS element is not released, is locked by status, or is otherwise invalid for posting.
steps: Identify the WBS element from the error and review its current status.
Check whether it is released for the intended posting and whether any user status blocks apply.
Verify validity, project assignment, and account assignment context.
Review whether settlement or closure statuses have made the WBS unavailable.
Correct the project status only if approved by the responsible team.
Retry the original process after the WBS is valid again.

title: Maintenance Order Not Released
description: A maintenance order exists but cannot proceed to planning, confirmation, or settlement because it has not been released.
steps: Open the maintenance order and check its current status.
Confirm whether the order is still only created and not yet released.
Review whether missing data or business approvals prevented release.
Complete any required planning or master data steps.
Release the order if authorized and appropriate.
Retest the blocked downstream process afterwards.

title: Maintenance Notification Incomplete
description: A maintenance notification exists but lacks required malfunction, cause, or technical object data, blocking follow-on processing or analysis.
steps: Open the notification and review incomplete or missing sections.
Check whether malfunction data, cause, coding, or technical object assignment is missing.
Confirm whether your process requires complete notification data before order creation or closure.
Maintain the missing notification information.
Save and validate the notification status.
Retest the downstream maintenance process afterwards.

title: Equipment or Functional Location Assignment Incorrect
description: Technical object data is inconsistent because equipment is not installed at the correct functional location or hierarchy position.
steps: Identify the equipment and expected functional location assignment.
Review current installation and structure data.
Check whether the issue came from master data, movement, or manual maintenance.
Correct the installation or location assignment using the proper technical object process.
Validate the final hierarchy and dependent data afterwards.
Retest the affected PM transaction or report.

title: Payroll Control Record Blocking Changes
description: HR master data changes are blocked because the payroll control record status does not allow modifications for the payroll area and period.
steps: Identify the employee's payroll area and the blocked master data action.
Check payroll control record status in PA03.
Confirm whether the payroll area is in a status that blocks master data changes.
Determine whether the change should be made through correction phase or after payroll completion.
Coordinate with payroll administrators before changing control record status.
Retry the HR change once the payroll status allows it.

title: HR Infotype Record Overlap / Time Constraint Error
description: An HR infotype change fails because the new record overlaps with an existing record in a way that violates time constraints.
steps: Capture the infotype, subtype, and employee involved.
Review the existing validity periods in PA20 or PA30.
Check whether the infotype has a time constraint that disallows overlapping records.
Correct the begin and end dates or delimit the old record properly.
Save the new record once the time-slice conflict is resolved.
Validate the final infotype history afterwards.

title: Holder Relationship Missing in OM
description: An employee exists and may have HR master data, but the Organizational Management holder relationship to a position is missing or incorrect.
steps: Identify the employee, position, and org unit that should be linked.
Check whether the employee is assigned as holder of the correct position in OM.
Compare PA master data with OM structure to see where the inconsistency sits.
Correct the holder relationship in the proper OM maintenance transaction.
Validate visibility in PPOME and related workflow or reporting scenarios.
Retest the affected downstream process afterwards.
